description: Science Y-goals progression map — descriptor codes, ACARA descriptions and Y-goals by year level. Used for prior knowledge lookups across the progression chain.

standards.title: Classification and Biodiversity
standards.description: Investigate the role of classification in ordering and organising the diversity of life on Earth and use and develop classification tools including dichotomous keys
standards.y_goal: Students explain why classification systems are used, apply and construct dichotomous keys, and evaluate how classification tools order and organise biodiversity.

standards.title: Food Webs and Ecosystems
standards.description: Use models, including food webs, to represent matter and energy flow in ecosystems and predict the impact of changing abiotic and biotic factors on populations
standards.y_goal: Students use food web models to represent matter and energy flow, and predict and explain population impacts of changes to abiotic and biotic factors.

standards.title: Earth-Sun-Moon System
standards.description: Model cyclic changes in the relative positions of the Earth, sun and moon and explain how these cycles cause eclipses and influence predictable phenomena on Earth, including seasons and tides
standards.y_goal: Students model and explain how cyclic changes in Earth-sun-moon positions cause eclipses, seasons and tides.

standards.title: Forces and Motion
standards.description: Investigate and represent balanced and unbalanced forces, including gravitational force, acting on objects, and relate changes in an object's motion to its mass and the magnitude and direction of forces acting on it
standards.y_goal: Students represent and explain how balanced and unbalanced forces, including gravity, relate to changes in an object's motion in terms of mass, magnitude and direction.

standards.title: Particle Theory and Properties
standards.description: Use particle theory to describe the arrangement of particles in a substance, including the motion of and attraction between particles, and relate this to the properties of the substance
standards.y_goal: Students use particle theory to explain and predict the physical properties of substances in terms of particle arrangement, motion and attraction.

standards.title: Pure Substances and Mixtures
standards.description: Use a particle model to describe differences between pure substances and mixtures and apply understanding of properties of substances to separate mixtures
standards.y_goal: Students use a particle model to explain the difference between pure substances and mixtures, and select and apply appropriate separation techniques based on properties of substances.

standards.title: Habitats and Living Things
standards.description: Investigate the physical conditions of a habitat and analyse how the growth and survival of living things is affected by changing physical conditions
standards.y_goal: Students analyse how physical conditions of a habitat affect the growth and survival of living things, and explain the relationship between changing conditions and organism responses

standards.title: Earth, Planets and Cyclic Phenomena
standards.description: Describe the movement of Earth and other planets relative to the sun and model how Earth's tilt, rotation on its axis and revolution around the sun relate to cyclic observable phenomena, including variable day and night length
standards.y_goal: Students model and explain how Earth's tilt, rotation and revolution produce cyclic observable phenomena including variable day and night length

standards.title: Electrical Circuits and Energy
standards.description: Investigate the transfer and transformation of energy in electrical circuits, including the role of circuit components, insulators and conductors
standards.y_goal: Students explain how energy is transferred and transformed in electrical circuits and describe the role of components, insulators and conductors

standards.title: Reversible and Irreversible Changes
standards.description: Compare reversible changes, including dissolving and changes of state, and irreversible changes, including cooking and rusting that produce new substances
standards.y_goal: Students compare reversible and irreversible changes, explaining what distinguishes them and identifying evidence that new substances have been produced

standards.title: Structural Features and Survival
standards.description: Examine how particular structural features and behaviours of living things enable their survival in specific habitats
standards.y_goal: Students explain how specific structural features and behaviours of living things are adaptations that enable survival in particular habitats

standards.title: Earth Surface Changes
standards.description: Describe how weathering, erosion, transportation and deposition cause slow or rapid change to Earth's surface
standards.y_goal: Students describe how weathering, erosion, transportation and deposition change Earth's surface over slow and rapid timescales

standards.title: Light and Shadows
standards.description: Identify sources of light, recognise that light travels in a straight path and describe how shadows are formed and light can be reflected and refracted
standards.y_goal: Students explain that light travels in straight paths from sources and describe how shadows, reflection and refraction result from this

standards.title: Particle Model of Matter
standards.description: Explain observable properties of solids, liquids and gases by modelling the motion and arrangement of particles
standards.y_goal: Students use a particle model to explain the observable properties of solids, liquids and gases in terms of particle motion and arrangement

standards.title: Collaboration in Science
standards.description: Examine why advances in science are often the result of collaboration or build on the work of others
standards.y_goal: Students explain why scientific advances often depend on collaboration and building on prior work

standards.title: Science in Decision Making
standards.description: Investigate how scientific knowledge is used by individuals and communities to identify problems, consider responses and make decisions
standards.y_goal: Students explain how scientific knowledge informs individual and community problem identification, responses and decision making

standards.title: Food Chains and Habitats
standards.description: Explain the roles and interactions of consumers, producers and decomposers within a habitat and how food chains represent feeding relationships
standards.y_goal: Students explain the roles of consumers, producers and decomposers in a habitat and represent feeding relationships using food chains

standards.title: Water Cycle
standards.description: Identify sources of water and describe key processes in the water cycle, including movement of water through the sky, landscape and ocean; precipitation; evaporation; and condensation
standards.y_goal: Students describe the key processes of the water cycle including precipitation, evaporation and condensation and trace the movement of water through sky, landscape and ocean

standards.title: Forces and Motion
standards.description: Identify how forces can be exerted by one object on another and investigate the effect of frictional, gravitational and magnetic forces on the motion of objects
standards.y_goal: Students explain how frictional, gravitational and magnetic forces are exerted between objects and describe their effects on motion

standards.title: Properties of Materials
standards.description: Examine the properties of natural and made materials including fibres, metals, glass and plastics and consider how these properties influence their use
standards.y_goal: Students explain how the properties of natural and made materials determine their suitability for different uses

standards.title: Data and Scientific Explanations
standards.description: Examine how people use data to develop scientific explanations
standards.y_goal: Students explain how data is used to develop and support scientific explanations

standards.title: Science Solving Problems
standards.description: Consider how people use scientific explanations to meet a need or solve a problem
standards.y_goal: Students explain how scientific knowledge is applied to meet needs and solve real-world problems

standards.title: Living and Non-living Things
standards.description: Compare characteristics of living and non-living things and examine the differences between the life cycles of plants and animals
standards.y_goal: Students compare characteristics of living and non-living things and explain differences between plant and animal life cycles

standards.title: Soils, Rocks and Minerals
standards.description: Compare the observable properties of soils, rocks and minerals and investigate why they are important Earth resources
standards.y_goal: Students compare observable properties of soils, rocks and minerals and explain their importance as Earth resources

standards.title: Heat Energy and Temperature
standards.description: Identify sources of heat energy and examine how temperature changes when heat energy is transferred from one object to another
standards.y_goal: Students identify heat energy sources and explain how temperature changes when heat energy transfers between objects

standards.title: States of Matter and Heat
standards.description: Investigate the observable properties of solids and liquids and how adding or removing heat energy leads to a change of state
standards.y_goal: Students describe observable properties of solids and liquids and explain how adding or removing heat energy causes changes of state

standards.title: Data and Scientific Explanations
standards.description: Examine how people use data to develop scientific explanations
standards.y_goal: Students explain how data is used to develop scientific explanations

standards.title: Science Solving Problems
standards.description: Consider how people use scientific explanations to meet a need or solve a problem
standards.y_goal: Students explain how scientific knowledge is applied to meet needs and solve problems

standards.title: Earth in the Solar System
standards.description: Recognise Earth is a planet in the solar system and identify patterns in the changing position of the sun, moon, planets and stars in the sky
standards.y_goal: Students recognise Earth as a planet and identify patterns in the changing positions of the sun, moon, planets and stars

standards.title: Sound and Vibration
standards.description: Explore different actions to make sounds and how to make a variety of sounds, and recognise that sound energy causes objects to vibrate
standards.y_goal: Students explain that sound is produced by different actions and that sound energy causes objects to vibrate

standards.title: Physical Changes to Materials
standards.description: Recognise that materials can be changed physically without changing their material composition and explore the effect of different actions on materials including bending, twisting, stretching and breaking into smaller pieces
standards.y_goal: Students recognise that physical actions change the shape of materials without changing their composition

standards.title: Basic Needs of Living Things
standards.description: Identify the basic needs of plants and animals, including air, water, food or shelter, and describe how the places they live meet those needs
standards.y_goal: Students identify the basic needs of plants and animals and describe how their habitat meets those needs

standards.title: Daily and Seasonal Changes
standards.description: Describe daily and seasonal changes in the environment and explore how these changes affect everyday life
standards.y_goal: Students describe daily and seasonal environmental changes and explain how these affect everyday life

standards.title: Pushes and Pulls
standards.description: Describe pushes and pulls in terms of strength and direction and predict the effect of these forces on objects' motion and shape
standards.y_goal: Students describe pushes and pulls in terms of strength and direction and predict their effects on motion and shape

standards.title: Features of Plants and Animals
standards.description: Observe external features of plants and animals and describe ways they can be grouped based on these features
standards.y_goal: Students observe external features of plants and animals and group them based on those features

standards.title: How Objects Move
standards.description: Describe how objects move and how factors including their size, shape or material influence their movement
standards.y_goal: Students describe how objects move and explain how size, shape and material influence movement

standards.title: Materials and Their Properties
standards.description: Recognise that objects can be composed of different materials and describe the observable properties of those materials
standards.y_goal: Students recognise that objects are made of materials and describe the observable properties of those materials

standards.title: Cells as Basic Units
standards.description: Recognise cells as the basic units of living things, compare plant and animal cells, and describe the functions of specialised cell structures and organelles
standards.y_goal: Students recognise cells as the basic units of living things, compare plant and animal cells, and describe the functions of specialised structures and organelles

standards.title: Structure, Function and Organ Systems
standards.description: Analyse the relationship between structure and function of cells, tissues and organs in a plant and an animal organ system and explain how these systems enable survival of the individual
standards.y_goal: Students analyse structure-function relationships across cells, tissues and organs and explain how organ systems enable individual survival

standards.title: Plate Tectonics
standards.description: Investigate tectonic activity including the formation of geological features at divergent, convergent and transform plate boundaries and describe the scientific evidence for the theory of plate tectonics
standards.y_goal: Students explain geological features formed at plate boundaries and describe the evidence supporting plate tectonic theory

standards.title: Rock Cycle
standards.description: Describe the key processes of the rock cycle, including the timescales over which they occur, and examine how the properties of sedimentary, igneous and metamorphic rocks reflect their formation and influence their use
standards.y_goal: Students describe rock cycle processes and explain how rock properties reflect formation history and determine use

standards.title: Energy Transfer and Transformation
standards.description: Classify different types of energy as kinetic or potential and investigate energy transfer and transformations in simple systems
standards.y_goal: Students classify energy as kinetic or potential and explain energy transfers and transformations in simple systems

standards.title: Elements, Compounds and Mixtures
standards.description: Classify matter as elements, compounds or mixtures and compare different representations of these, including 2-dimensional and 3-dimensional models, symbols for elements and formulas for molecules and compounds
standards.y_goal: Students classify matter as elements, compounds or mixtures and use symbols, formulas and models to represent them

standards.title: Physical and Chemical Changes
standards.description: Compare physical and chemical changes and identify indicators of energy change in chemical reactions
standards.y_goal: Students compare physical and chemical changes and identify indicators of energy change in chemical reactions

standards.title: Body Systems and Negative Feedback
standards.description: Compare the role of body systems in regulating and coordinating the body's response to a stimulus, and describe the operation of a negative feedback mechanism
standards.y_goal: Students explain how body systems coordinate responses to stimuli and describe the operation of negative feedback mechanisms

standards.title: Reproduction and Species Survival
standards.description: Describe the form and function of reproductive cells and organs in animals and plants, and analyse how the processes of sexual and asexual reproduction enable survival of the species
standards.y_goal: Students describe reproductive structures and processes in animals and plants and analyse how sexual and asexual reproduction enable species survival

standards.title: Carbon Cycle and Earth's Spheres
standards.description: Represent the carbon cycle and examine how key processes including combustion, photosynthesis and respiration rely on interactions between Earth's spheres
standards.y_goal: Students represent the carbon cycle and explain how combustion, photosynthesis and respiration depend on interactions between Earth's spheres

standards.title: Wave and Particle Models
standards.description: Use wave and particle models to describe energy transfer through different mediums and examine the usefulness of each model for explaining phenomena
standards.y_goal: Students use wave and particle models to describe energy transfer and evaluate the usefulness of each model for explaining different phenomena

standards.title: Conservation of Energy and Efficiency
standards.description: Apply the law of conservation of energy to analyse system efficiency in terms of energy inputs, outputs, transfers and transformations
standards.y_goal: Students apply conservation of energy to analyse system efficiency in terms of energy inputs, outputs, transfers and transformations

standards.title: Atomic Model and Radioactive Decay
standards.description: Explain how the model of the atom changed following the discovery of electrons, protons and neutrons and describe how natural radioactive decay results in stable atoms
standards.y_goal: Students explain how atomic theory developed through discovery of subatomic particles and describe how radioactive decay produces stable atoms

standards.title: Chemical Equations and Conservation of Mass
standards.description: Model the rearrangement of atoms in chemical reactions using a range of representations, including word and simple balanced chemical equations, and use these to demonstrate the law of conservation of mass
standards.y_goal: Students model atom rearrangement in chemical reactions using word and balanced equations and demonstrate conservation of mass

standards.title: Genetics and Inheritance
standards.description: Explain the role of meiosis and mitosis and the function of chromosomes, DNA and genes in heredity and predict patterns of Mendelian inheritance
standards.y_goal: Students explain the roles of meiosis, mitosis, chromosomes, DNA and genes in heredity and predict Mendelian inheritance patterns

standards.title: Evolution by Natural Selection
standards.description: Use the theory of evolution by natural selection to explain past and present diversity and analyse the scientific evidence supporting the theory
standards.y_goal: Students use natural selection theory to explain biological diversity and analyse the evidence supporting evolution

standards.title: Big Bang Theory
standards.description: Describe how the big bang theory models the origin and evolution of the universe and analyse the supporting evidence for the theory
standards.y_goal: Students describe how the big bang theory models the universe's origin and evolution and analyse the supporting evidence

standards.title: Global Climate Change
standards.description: Use models of energy flow between the geosphere, biosphere, hydrosphere and atmosphere to explain patterns of global climate change
standards.y_goal: Students use energy flow models between Earth's spheres to explain patterns of global climate change

standards.title: Newton's Laws of Motion
standards.description: Investigate Newton's laws of motion and quantitatively analyse the relationship between force, mass and acceleration of objects
standards.y_goal: Students apply Newton's laws of motion and quantitatively analyse the relationship between force, mass and acceleration

standards.title: Periodic Table and Atomic Structure
standards.description: Explain how the structure and properties of atoms relate to the organisation of the elements in the periodic table
standards.y_goal: Students explain how atomic structure and properties determine the organisation of elements in the periodic table

standards.title: Reaction Types and Rates
standards.description: Identify patterns in synthesis, decomposition and displacement reactions and investigate the factors that affect reaction rates
standards.y_goal: Students identify patterns in synthesis, decomposition and displacement reactions and explain factors that affect reaction rates

progression_threads.description: Conceptual progression trees from Foundation to Year 10, organised by science strand. Foundation standards are roots; children represent the next conceptual step in the strand.
